response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of the GISCollective geo-data platform, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and relevant low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial settings, and his commercial C++ exposure is brief and dated (~7 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015), with no Rust and no routing or navigation pipeline experience. Recent years have been spent in TypeScript/Node.js web development, so a systems-level routing tiling role would be a significant pivot, though his systems fundamentals and mapping background make him worth a closer look if the team can support ramp-up.
strengths: Real geospatial domain experience: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching, microservices at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker/Kubernetes, and open-source tooling contributions
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); no Rust experience
No experience building routing engines, map-data/tiling pipelines, or navigation guidance systems
Recent focus (2020-present) is web/full-stack TypeScript work, far from systems-level data pipeline engineering
